questions.original_question: 路特斯科技2024年前9个月的业绩表现如何？
questions.answer: 在2024年的前9个月，路特斯科技（Lotus Technology）表现非常强劲，共交付了超过7,600辆汽车，同比增长136%。全球交付量分布相对均衡，欧洲、中国、北美和世界其他地区各占总交付量的18-35%。欧洲市场的交付量同比增长了372%，而世界其他地区的交付量也实现了110%的增长。此外，路特斯科技在2024年前9个月的总收入达到6.53亿美元，同比增长105%，其中商品销售收入为6.24亿美元，服务收入为2900万美元。尽管毛利率从2023年同期的11%下降至9%，但服务收入的毛利率显著提升至55%，主要得益于高利润率的技术相关收入增长。以上信息基于2025年1月15日之前的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

rag_info.chunk_content: In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered over 7,600 vehicles, marking a 136% increase compared to the same period in 2023, when 3,221 vehicles were delivered. The intelligent driving business showed remarkable growth, with revenue from external customers reaching $11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%. Despite this, the gross profit margins declined, with Q3 2024 margins at 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. For the first nine months of 2024, the gross profit margin was 9%, compared to 11% in the same period of 2023.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024**

In the first nine months of 2024, the Company achieved significant growth, delivering a total of 7,617 units, representing a remarkable 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Deliveries were well-distributed across various regions, with each contributing between 18% to 35% to the overall total, supported by an extensive network of over 200 stores globally. The European market stood out with an exceptional 372% YoY increase in deliveries, while the Rest of the World experienced a 110% growth. Notably, new deliveries commenced for the Emira model in South Africa and India, and the Eletre model in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. Additionally, the unveiling of the Eletre Carbon variant for North America is anticipated, with deliveries expected to start in the first half of 2025.

Sales figures for the period included 3,983 units of "Lifestyle SUV and Sedan," reflecting a 126% increase from 1,762 units in 2023, and 3,634 units of "Sportscars," which marked a 149% rise from 1,459 units in the previous year. Regionally, Europe accounted for 2,683 units (35% share) in 2024, a significant increase from 568 units (18% share) in 2023. China recorded 1,880 units (25% share), a slight decline from 1,993 units (62% share) in 2023. North America reported 1,665 units (22% share) with no prior data for comparison, while the Rest of the World achieved 1,389 units (18% share), up from 660 units (20% share) in 2023.

Financially, the Company reported revenue of 19 units for the first nine months of 2024, representing a substantial 217% YoY increase from 6 units in 2023. Revenue from Lotus increased to 8 units, reflecting a 100% rise, while revenue from other customers surged to 11 units, a remarkable 450% increase from 2 units in 2023. Furthermore, Lotus Robotics secured contracts valued at approximately $130 million for intelligent driving solutions with various automakers, significantly enhancing its technological footprint within the industry.

Overall, the first nine months of 2024 demonstrated the Company's robust growth trajectory, marked by increased deliveries, expanded market presence, and substantial revenue growth, positioning it favorably for future developments.

title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand – Historical Achievements of Lotus Technology Inc.
- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

(“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology Inc. also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology Inc. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered approximately 2,194 vehicles, with global markets outside China contributing over 75% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology
Reports Unaudited First Quarter 2024 Financial Result 
summary: Lotus Tech, a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, reported impressive financial results for the first quarter of 2024, with revenues reaching $173 million, reflecting an 811% year-on-year growth and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered 2,194 vehicles, with over 75% of these sales occurring in global markets outside of China. This quarter marked the launch of the Emeya, Lotus's first electric hyper-grand tourer, and the introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service, which offers premium customization options for its vehicles. The company's direct-to-customer sales model in China led to over 100% growth in deliveries, while the Emira, a traditional sports car, began deliveries in the US. With a robust global sales network of over 200 stores, Lotus Tech is expanding its reach into new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas, and plans to introduce additional models throughout the year. CEO Qingfeng Feng emphasized the company's commitment to innovation and its Vision80 plan, highlighting the effectiveness of its global strategy in driving growth in key luxury markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: With a global sales network of over 200 stores in prime locations around the world, Lotus Technology continues to leverage its global distribution footprint for international expansion. After China and Europe, Lotus Technology began taking orders for the Eletre in more than 10 new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas in the first quarter. Lotus Technology also expects to begin expanding Emeya sales to new markets in the third quarter of the year. "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering commitment to innovation and excellence, Lotus Technology is making steady progress on delivering Lotus Tech’s Vision80 plan," said Lotus Technology CEO Qingfeng Feng. metadata.title_summary:
title: Financial Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Financial Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024**

For the first nine months of 2024, the company achieved total revenues of $653 million, reflecting a remarkable 105% increase compared to $318 million during the same period in 2023. This growth was primarily driven by sales of goods, which totaled $624 million, marking a 104% year-over-year increase. Additionally, service revenues reached $29 million, representing a significant 129% increase from the previous year.

Despite the impressive revenue growth, the company experienced a decline in gross margin, which fell to 9% from 11% in 2023. The gross margin for goods sold specifically decreased to 7%, down from 10% in the prior year, largely due to destocking activities. In contrast, the gross margin for service revenues improved significantly to 55%, up from 28% in 2023, attributed to a rise in high-margin technology-related income.

The operating loss for the first nine months of 2024 was $598 million, an 18% increase from the $508 million loss reported in the same period of 2023. The net loss for the period also increased by 27%, totaling $667 million compared to $527 million in the previous year. When excluding share-based compensation expenses, the adjusted net loss (non-GAAP) was $633 million, reflecting a 20% year-over-year increase. Furthermore, the adjusted EBITDA (non-GAAP) indicated a loss of $563 million, which is a 16% increase in losses compared to the $486 million loss recorded in 2023.

In summary, while the company demonstrated substantial revenue growth in the first nine months of 2024, it concurrently faced escalating operating and net losses, alongside a decline in gross margins for goods sold.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus achieved a 136% year-on-year increase in deliveries, maintaining its position as a top-performing luxury car brand among global competitors. The company delivered over 7,600 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, making it the top seller among British luxury car brands. Four models were in delivery, each with an average Manufacturer’s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) over $100,000. The current portfolio includes one SUV, one sedan, and two sportscars. Lotus maintained the MSRP above $100,000 throughout the first nine months of 2024, despite faster-than-sector growth and intensified competition. The intelligent driving business achieved a contract value of $130 million. Lotus Robotics, the intelligent driving division, provided solutions to multiple global auto conglomerates, contributing significantly to this growth. Lotus's global expansion included entering over 10 new markets across the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries, Asia, and Oceania. The company introduced the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, and also penetrated the fleet market for environmentally sustainable corporate clients in Europe.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered over 7,600 vehicles, marking a 136% increase compared to the same period in 2023, when 3,221 vehicles were delivered. The third quarter alone saw 2,744 vehicles delivered, a 54% increase over the 1,782 vehicles delivered in Q3 2023. Total revenue for the first nine months of 2024 amounted to $653 million, representing an impressive 105% year-on-year growth from the $318 million reported in the same period of 2023. In the third quarter of 2024, revenue was $255 million, showing a 36% increase from the $188 million in Q3 2023. The intelligent driving business showed remarkable growth, with revenue from external customers reaching $11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%. Despite this, the gross profit margins declined, with Q3 2024 margins at 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. For the first nine months of 2024, the gross profit margin was 9%, compared to 11% in the same period of 2023.

● Delivery  
Updates: Lotus delivered a total of 8,631 vehicles in the first 10 months of 2024.  
● New Technology  
Launched: Lotus unveiled its "Hyper Hybrid EV technology" at the Guangzhou Auto Show. The 900V Hyper Hybrid EV technology features a Hybrid Electric Drivetrain and dual Hyper Charging technology: Ultra-Fast Plug-In Charging and Ultra-Fast On-The-Drive Charging, both with industry-leading charging speeds. The Hyper Hybrid technology enables high performance, high efficiency, and a flexible longer driving distance of over 1,100 kilometers.  
● Delivery of Urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) Service and OTA: Lotus started open beta testing for urban NOA in key cities in October 2024, following the launch of highway OTA services. Certain OTA features, including the highway assistant driving function, have been launched in Europe.  
● Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024: In October, Lotus was awarded the Highly Commended honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Recent Developments 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Recent Developments**

In the first ten months of 2024, the Company achieved significant milestones, delivering a total of 8,631 vehicles. A highlight of this period was the launch of its innovative "Hyper Hybrid EV technology" at the Guangzhou Auto Show. This cutting-edge technology features a 900V Hybrid Electric Drivetrain and dual Hyper Charging capabilities, enabling ultra-fast plug-in and on-the-drive charging with industry-leading speeds. As a result, the vehicles equipped with this technology can achieve an impressive driving range of over 1,100 kilometers, showcasing both high performance and efficiency.

In addition to advancements in vehicle technology, the Company initiated an open beta test for its urban Navigate on Autopilot (NOA) service in key cities in October 2024. This rollout follows the successful introduction of highway over-the-air (OTA) services, which have also been implemented in Europe, further enhancing the Company's technological offerings.

The Company's commitment to sustainability has been recognized with the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, distinguishing it as the only automotive industry awardee in this category. This accolade underscores the Company's dedication to integrating sustainable practices within its operations and product offerings. 

Overall, these developments reflect the Company's strategic focus on innovation, sustainability, and enhanced customer experience in the rapidly evolving automotive landscape.

title: Results of Operations 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Financial Performance Overview**

This summary provides an integrated analysis of the financial performance for the first six months of 2024 and 2023, alongside annual data for 2023, 2022, and 2021, with all figures expressed in thousands of US dollars.

**Revenues:**
In the first half of 2024, total revenues reached $398,115, with $382,893 derived from sales of goods and $15,222 from service revenues. This marks a substantial increase compared to the first half of 2023, where total revenues were $130,035, comprised of $124,854 in goods and $5,181 in services. For the full year of 2023, total revenues amounted to $679,008, including $660,158 from goods and $18,850 from services. Historical data shows revenues of $9,557 in 2022 and $3,687 in 2021.

**Costs:**
The total cost of revenues for the first half of 2024 was $347,203, with $340,882 attributed to goods and $6,321 to services. This is a significant increase from the first half of 2023, which reported total costs of $123,908 ($119,557 for goods and $4,351 for services). For the full year 2023, total costs reached $564,741, while prior years recorded costs of $12,086 in 2022 and $6,302 in 2021.

**Gross Profit:**
Gross profit for the first six months of 2024 was $50,912, a notable increase from $6,127 in 2023. For the entire year of 2023, gross profit totaled $102,181, with 2022 and 2021 showing gross profits of $2,307 and $557, respectively.

**Operating Expenses:**
Total operating expenses for the first half of 2024 were $488,618, compared to $350,539 in 2023. For the year 2023, operating expenses amounted to $838,120, while 2022 and 2021 reported expenses of $699,720 and $113,499, respectively.

**Operating Loss:**
The operating loss for the first six months of 2024 was $437,706, an increase from $344,412 in 2023. Annual operating losses were recorded at $735,939 in 2023, $687,413 in 2022, and $112,942 in 2021.

**Interest and Investment Income:**
Interest expenses for the first half of 2024 totaled $11,708, compared to $3,470 in 2023. Interest income for the same periods was $8,658 in 2024 and $5,848 in 2023. Investment income, which includes foreign currency exchange gains, was $3,496 in 2024, contrasting with a loss of $4,429, compared to gains of $2,770 and losses of $3,619 in 2023.

**Net Loss:**
Loss before income taxes for the first half of 2024 was $459,897, compared to $352,960 in 2023. The annual net losses were $749,142 in 2023, $724,563 in 2022, and $108,678 in 2021. After accounting for income tax expenses, the net loss for the first six months of 2024 was $460,252, compared to $352,942 in 2023. The annual net losses were $750,255 in 2023, $724,563 in 2022, and $110,531 in 2021.

The financial results also note the applicable income tax rates for subsidiaries located in the U.K., Netherlands, and Germany. Additionally, the use of adjusted net loss and adjusted EBITDA as non-GAAP financial measures is highlighted for performance evaluation.
